searches.keyword: US Navy seizes Iranian cargo ship Touska Gulf of Oman April 19 2026

results.title: US Navy seizes Iranian cargo ship 'Touska' in Gulf of Oman after hours-long standoff
results.snippet: USS Spruance intercepted sanctioned Iranian vessel Touska in Gulf of Oman, firing on engine room after 6 hours of warnings. CENTCOM confirms ship was under US Treasury sanctions.

results.title: Trump announces seizure of Iranian ship on Truth Social: 'We got another one'
results.snippet: President Trump posted on Truth Social about the seizure of the Touska, calling it proof of Iran's sanctions evasion.

results.title: Iran condemns US seizure of Touska as 'armed piracy and theft'
results.snippet: Iran's foreign ministry called the US Navy seizure of the Touska 'armed piracy and theft' and threatened retaliation. IRGC warned of 'decisive response'.

results.title: CENTCOM: Seized Iranian ship Touska was under Treasury sanctions for prior illegal activity
results.snippet: US Central Command confirmed the Touska had been designated under US Treasury sanctions for prior involvement in illicit cargo transfers.

results.title: US Navy fires on Iranian cargo ship engine room during Gulf of Oman seizure
results.snippet: USS Spruance fired on the engine room of the Touska after the vessel refused to stop for 6 hours. No casualties reported.

results.title: Iranian ship Touska seized: What we know about the Gulf of Oman standoff
results.snippet: The USS Spruance intercepted the Touska after it ignored repeated warnings. The ship was sanctioned by the US Treasury.

results.title: Iran refuses to return to Pakistan-mediated talks with US as blockade continues
results.snippet: Iran's rejection of the second round of talks leaves diplomatic channels in limbo with the ceasefire expiring Wednesday.

results.title: Second round of US-Iran talks in jeopardy after Tehran says it won't attend
results.snippet: Iran's refusal throws second round of indirect US-Iran talks into uncertainty just days before ceasefire expiration.

results.title: Trump escalates rhetoric: threatens 'every Power Plant' and 'every Bridge' in Iran

results.title: Analysis: Trump's infrastructure threats raise questions about post-ceasefire plans
results.snippet: Military analysts note Trump's threat to destroy every power plant and bridge in Iran would represent a massive escalation beyond current operations.

results.title: European allies express alarm at Trump's Iran infrastructure threats

results.snippet: Since becoming IRGC Commander-in-Chief on March 1, 2026, Ahmad Vahidi has become the dominant figure in Iranian politics, blocking even the president's cabinet appointments.

results.snippet: IRGC Commander-in-Chief Ahmad Vahidi reportedly blocked President Pezeshkian's choice for intelligence minister, demonstrating the military's growing control over civilian government.

searches.keyword: Sanmar Herald audio released crew recording Iran Hormuz April 2026

results.title: Sanmar Herald audio: 'You gave me clearance to go. You are firing now. Let me turn back'
results.snippet: Released audio reveals crew of the Sanmar Herald pleading with Iranian forces: 'You gave me clearance to go. My name is second on your list. You are firing now. Let me turn back.'

results.title: Audio from Sanmar Herald reveals chaos of Iran's Hormuz attack
results.snippet: Newly released audio captures the moment Iranian forces opened fire on the Indian VLCC Sanmar Herald despite having cleared it to transit. Ship was carrying ~2 million barrels of Iraqi crude to India.

results.title: Second ship Jag Arnav also attacked in Hormuz; departed from Saudi Arabia's al-Jubail
results.snippet: The Jag Arnav, which departed from Saudi Arabia's al-Jubail port, was also fired upon. Together with the Sanmar Herald, at least two vessels were attacked.

results.title: India demands accountability after Sanmar Herald audio reveals IRGC opened fire despite clearance
results.snippet: India's foreign ministry called for accountability after audio proved the Sanmar Herald had received clearance from Iranian forces before being fired upon.

results.title: Sanmar Herald was carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude when Iran attacked
results.snippet: The VLCC Sanmar Herald was carrying approximately 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude oil bound for India when IRGC forces opened fire.

results.snippet: Israel has established a 10km 'Yellow Line' military zone in southern Lebanon, declaring 55 villages off-limits. IDF razing villages during ceasefire, replicating 'Gaza model in Lebanon'.

results.title: At least 773 killed in Lebanon since ceasefire started, more than 2,000 wounded
results.snippet: Lebanon's health ministry reports at least 773 people killed and more than 2,000 wounded since the ceasefire began, as Israel continues operations inside the 'Yellow Line'.

results.snippet: More than 40,000 homes destroyed or damaged in southern Lebanon. The IDF's 'Yellow Line' prevents residents from returning to 55 villages.

results.title: UN calls Israel's 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon a violation of ceasefire agreement
results.snippet: United Nations officials called Israel's establishment of a 10km military zone in Lebanon a clear violation of ceasefire terms.
